Associated problems: Primary malignant neoplasm of colon (disorder) * (C18.7)
ATTENTION: This report may contain Protected Health Information as defined by HIPAA and should be managed in accordance
with your organization's policies for Protected Health Information.
Primary malignant neoplasm of colon (disorder) ( Stage Date: 10/05/2021, Stage IIIC (T4a, N2a, McM0)-
Date of Dx:9/2021 ; ICD-10:C18.7 Malignant neoplasm of sigmoid colon
Ms. Rodriguez is a pleasant 43 year old woman with colon cancer. Patient underwent colonoscopy on 9/8/21 and was
found to have a mass at 35 cm. Biopsy was positive for adenocarcinoma. She underwent total proctocolectomy and
ileostomy on 9/21/21. Pathology was reported as adenocarcinoma, 3.5 X 2 cm, Grade 2, negative margins, negative for
lymphatic, vascular and perineural invasion, 6/36 positive nodes, 3 tumor deposits and was staged as T4a N2a. Post
op, she developed pneumomediastinum which resolved on its own. She was started on FOLFOX on 10/20/21. She
developed left arm pain and left jaw pain on second day of 5-FU infusion. Due to concern for possible coronary
vasospasm, her pump was discontinued. Subsequently, she was able to tolerate chemotherapy. She completed 12
cycles on 5/16/22. Oxaliplatin was held in last 2 cycles due to neuropathy. She underwent ileostomy reversal on 7/28/
22. She was admitted at Southern Hills hospital on 8/31/22 with bowel obstruction. At that time, she was also found to
have a pelvic mass. She was evaluated by Dr Kushnir and underwent surgery at Valley hospital on 9/7/22. She was
found to have large pelvic mass in relation to ovary and bowel loops. Biopsy was positive for colon cancer. She was
admitted at Southern hills hospital and underwent surgery by Dr Rives on 9/20/22. He was able to resect the mass and
performed another ileostomy. Pathology was reported as colon cancer. She was started on FOLFIRI and Bevacizumab
on 12/12/22 and she completed 11 cycles. Subsequently, she underwent PET scan and MRI pelvis in 08/23 and was
found to have large 13 cm pelvic mass c/w recurrent disease. She underwent hysterectomy left oophorectomy and
bilateral salpingectomy on 8/17/23. Pathology did show metastatic colon cancer in left ovary. There were serosal
metastatic implants on uterus. The patient underwent evaluation at Mayo Clinics. MRI of abdomen did show single liver
metastases and evidence of peritoneal disease. She was started on FOLFOXIRI on 10/16/23. She went to Mayo Clinics
in 01/24 and was advised to continue chemotherapy for 3 more months. She underwent HipEC and debulking on 7/11/
24 at Mayo Clinic. She was advised to continue FOLFOX and Bevacizumab and she completed 4 cycles. She had MRI
done at Mayo Clinics. As per patient, she has been advised to continue on 5-FU/Leucovorin and Bevacizumab. She was
found to have stomal obstruction in 11/24 due to tumor and required surgery. PET scan showed disease progression
and she was started on Lonsurf and Bevacizumab in 02/25 and could not tolerate it. Subsequently, she was placed on
re challenging with FOLFIRINOX and progressed. Patient completed palliative radiation to Abdominal wall metastasis.
She is currently on fruquintinib. This is a KRAS G12D mutated progressive colon cancer.
01/21/2026: Patient reports not feeling well since last visit. She discontinued fruquintinib with last dose on 01/14/2026
due to GI bleeding. Patient underwent endoscopic evaluation of stump which revealed stump infection and was
diagnosed with ulcerative colitis. She was prescribed antibiotics for inflammation by gastroenterology. Patient
continues to have intermittent bleeding episodes and reports ongoing pain.
General: no weight loss, no fever, no chilis, no night sweats, energy level good.
Eyes: no diplopia, no transient or permanent loss of vision, no scotomata.
ENT/Mouth: no tinnitus, normal hearing, no epistaxis, no hoarseness, no oral ulcers, no gingival bleeding, no sore
throat, no postnasal drip, no mouth pain, no sinus pain, no dysphagia.
Cardiovascular: no chest pain, no palpitations, no syncope, no upper extremity edema, no lower extremity edema, no
Respiratory: no cough, no hemoptysis, no pleurisy, no wheezing.
Gastrointestinal: reports bleeding, no abdominal pain, no nausea, no vomiting, no constipation, no hematochezia, no
jaundice, no abnormal cramping, no hematemesis, no diarrhea, no melena, no dyspepsia, no dysphagia.
Musculoskeletal: no muscle pain, no swollen joints
redness, no bone pain, no spine tenderness.
Skin: no rash, no nodules, no pruritus, no lesions.
Neurologic: no confusion, no seizures, no syncope,
abnormal gait, no weakness, no sensory changes.
no tremor, no speech change, no headache, no hiccups, no
Psychiatric: no depression, no anxiety, concentration normal.
Endocrine: no polyuria, no polydipsia, no thyroid disease symptoms, no hot flashes.
Hematologic: no epistaxis, no gingival bleeding, no petechiae, no ecchymosis.
Allergy/Immunologic: no eczema, no frequent mucous membrane infections, no frequent respiratory
recurrent urticaria, no frequent skin infections.
Blood pressure: 100/84, Sitting, R arm, Regular, Pulse: 88, Temperature: 97.9 F, Respirations: 18, O2 sat: 97%, At Rest,
Room Air, Pain Scale: 0, Height: 63 in, Weight: 100.2 lb, BSA: 1.44, BMI: 17.75 kg/m2
General: No pallor. No palpable lymphadenopathy.
Neurological: Alert and oriented to time, place, and person. Pupils equally round and reactive to light. Extraocular
movements normal. Gait is normal. Speech is normal.
HEENT: Pharynx normal. No hoarseness. No sinus tenderness.
Ear, nose, and throat exam: Normal.
Cardiovascular: Regular. No murmur. No cardiomegaly.
Extremities: No pedal edema. No calf tenderness.
CT AP done 12/18/2025: Again seen are dilated loops of bowel filled with fluid in the pelvis. Consider bowel
obstruction. Multiple surgical anastomotic staple lines are present in the loops of bowel. There is a right lower quadrant
ostomy. Consider adhesions with other etiologies not excluded. This is similar to previous exam. Multiple
hypoenhancing masses in the liver and numerous varying size lung nodules are present. These are concerning for
metastatic disease. These are similar to previous exam.
NM bone scan done 11/25/2025: No definite bony metastatic disease identified.
Pathology done 12/23/2025: A. REctum, proximal biopsy: Rectal mucosa with mild proctitis and reactive/
inflammatory changes. Negative for dysplasia or malignancy. B. Rectum, distal, biopsy: Rectal mudosa with mild
proctitis and reactive/inflammatory changes. Negative for dysplasia or malignancy.
1. Ms. Rodriguez is a 44-year-old woman with KRAS G12D mutated, MSI stable, HER2 negative metastatic colorectal
cancer, T4a N2a. Disease primarily involves peritoneum, abdominal wall, liver, and subcutaneous tissue. She has been
on sequential lines of therapy including FOLFOX, FOLFIRI with Bevacizumab, FOLFOXIRI, and Lonsurf with
Bevacizumab. Patient has failed four lines of therapy. Patient started fruquintinib as fifth-line anti-VEGF therapy as
bridging treatment until G12D directed clinical trials become available. 01/21/2026: Patient discontinued fruquintinib
on 01/14/2026 due to GI bleeding. Current labs show improvement with WBC 4.1, hemoglobin 12.0, platelets 138.
Patient scheduled for tertiary care consultation at Mayo Clinic on 02/04/2026 for further treatment options.
2. Iron deficiency anemia with hemoglobin stable at 12.0. Patient previously received iron infusion with continued
3. Mild leukopenia resolved with WBC improved to 4.1 from previous 2.9.
4. Ulcerative colitis with stump inflammation diagnosed by gastroenterology via endoscopy. Patient prescribed
1. Discontinue fruquintinib due to GI bleeding and stump complications with ulcerative colitis flare.
2. Initiate steroid therapy for ulcerative colitis flare-up in stump area as recommended by gastroenterology.
episode. 3. Plan to restart fruquintinib at reduced frequency of every other day as tolerated after resolution of current bleeding
4. Tertiary care consultation scheduled at Mayo Clinic on 02/04/2026 for input on further treatment options and
5. Continue coordination for G12D directed clinical trial options.
6. Monitor complete blood count and iron studies given history of anemia.
7. Follow up in 2 weeks to reassess treatment tolerance and response to steroid therapy.
Treatment plan is consistent with current NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology.
- Patient discontinued fruquintinib on 01/14/2026 due to GI bleeding
- New diagnosis of ulcerative colitis with stump inflammation managed with antibiotics and steroids
- Laboratory values show improvement in white blood cell count and stable hemoglobin
- Tertiary care consultation at Mayo Clinic scheduled for 02/04/2026
- Plan to restart fruquintinib at reduced frequency of every other day as tolerated
- Follow up in 2 weeks to assess treatment tolerance and response
